2605.18003.title: Spiker-LL: An Energy-Efficient FPGA Accelerator Enabling Adaptive Local Learning in Spiking Neural Networks
2605.18003.paper_summary: Deploying adaptive intelligence at the edge remains challenging due to the high computational and energy cost of training neural models. Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) offer a promising alternative, but enabling on-device learning requires hardware-algorithm co-design. This paper presents SPIKER-LL, an FPGA-based SNN accelerator that extends the open-source Spiker+ inference architecture with efficient support for the STSF local learning rule. Through targeted microarchitectural extensions, SPIKER-LL performs inference and online learning with minimal overhead. Across MNIST, F-MNIST, and DIGITS, it achieves up to 93% accuracy, sub-millisecond latency, and less than 0.1 mJ per inference, while remaining DSP-free and highly scalable for edge-FPGA deployments.
2605.18003.paper_summary_zh: 在邊緣部署自適應智能仍然具有挑戰性，因為訓練神經模型的計算和能量成本很高。脈衝神經網絡（SNNs）提供了一個有前景的替代方案，但實現設備上的學習需要硬體與算法的共同設計。本文提出了SPIKER-LL，一種基於FPGA的SNN加速器，擴展了開源的Spiker+推理架構，並有效支持STSF局部學習規則。通過針對性的微架構擴展，SPIKER-LL以最小的開銷執行推理和在線學習。在MNIST、F-MNIST和DIGITS上，它達到了最高93%的準確率、亞毫秒的延遲和每次推理少於0.1 mJ的能耗，同時保持無DSP並且對邊緣FPGA部署具有高度可擴展性。
2605.18003.author: Alessio Caviglia et.al.
2605.18003.authors: Alessio Caviglia, Filippo Marostica, Alessandro Savino, Stefano Di Carlo
